message:
feat: Add TcpPoolDO for persistent TCP connections across trampoline rounds

TcpPoolDO is a Durable Object that holds real TCP connections via
cloudflare:sockets. Connections persist in memory between trampoline
rounds (70-140s idle timeout), eliminating the need to replay full
TCP conversations each round.

Key changes:
- TcpPoolDO class with connect/send/recv/close + replayOps (batch)
- replayTcpOpsViaDO routes ops to DO instances keyed by host:port
- Multiple host:port connections fan out to separate DOs in parallel
- Falls back to raw socket replay when TCP_POOL binding is absent
- wrangler.toml updated with DO binding and compat_date 2024-04-03

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 name = "pymode"
 main = "src/worker.ts"
-compatibility_date = "2024-01-01"
+compatibility_date = "2024-04-03"
 
 [limits]
 cpu_ms = 30000
@@ -19,3 +19,14 @@ globs = ["**/*.zip"]
 # [[kv_namespaces]]
 # binding = "PYMODE_DATA"
 # id = "YOUR_KV_NAMESPACE_ID"
+
+# Durable Object for persistent TCP connections (database drivers, etc.)
+# Connections survive across trampoline rounds — no conversation replay needed.
+[durable_objects]
+bindings = [
+  { name = "TCP_POOL", class_name = "TcpPoolDO" },
+]
+
+[[migrations]]
+tag = "v1"
+new_classes = ["TcpPoolDO"]
